action.reasoning: Observation: the decoupled two-stage approach improved balanced accuracy to 0.8011, with many-shot accuracy rising strongly but few-shot still stuck around 0.664. Analysis: representation quality seems adequate; the remaining bottleneck is classifier bias toward head classes. A low-risk, high-value next step is post-hoc classifier calibration using class-prior-aware logit adjustment at inference, tuned on the balanced validation set. This is cheap to train/evaluate, preserves the strong backbone, and often improves balanced accuracy in long-tailed settings. Decision: update predict.py to support an optional inference-time logit adjustment stored in the checkpoint, extend train.py with a calibration mode that searches tau on val logits after stage-2 training and saves the best tau into the checkpoint, then retrain the same two-stage pipeline with calibration, smoke-test predict, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 64 --lr 3e-4 --weight_decay 5e-4 --loss_name ce --label_smoothing 0.0 --checkpoint_out checkpoints/stage1.pt --num_workers 4 && python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --lr 1e-3 --weight_decay 1e-4 --loss_name cb_ce --use_weighted_sampler --freeze_backbone --resume checkpoints/stage1.pt --checkpoint_out checkpoints/best.pt --num_workers 4 --calibrate_infer_logit && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output pred_smoke.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('pred_smoke.json')); print(len(d['predictions']), d['predictions'][:5])"
